name: Kopiku Indonesian Coffee
address: 1443 Lombard St, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA
aiSummary: Casual coffeehouse highlighting single-origin Indonesian coffees, plus roasts, pastries, and toast.
review: Pandan Matcha - This one was our favorite. It leans more on the sweeter side but it was perfectly balanced with the grassy matcha and I loved the aromatics that came from the pandan. A great mix of earthiness and creaminess.
note: Kopiku's rating is driven by their unique Indonesian coffee flavors, not the matcha. That said, their pandan matcha latte is one-of-a-kind.

name: Junbi Matcha & Tea
address: 685 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA
review: The matcha is not too strong, but tastes perfect. Love the music and decor. Both the Yuzu Dragon Fruit Refresher and Cold Guava Matcha Latte tasted delicious! I couldn't choose one over the other.
note: Possibly the most adventurous matcha menu in the city, pairing matcha with unexpected flavors like guava, blueberry lavender, and Madagascar vanilla.

name: The Wild Fox
address: 123 Battery St, San Francisco, CA 94111, USA
review:
I found a spot facing the side window next to another guy on a laptop. 

I figured I would park there for about an hour while I enjoyed my iced mango matcha drink. 

It was super tasty and I can confirm that everything everyone has said is true.
note: Opened November 2025, the Matcha Mango Shizuku is the most popular drink. Pair it with a chashu or egg salad sandwich.

name: Tadaima Inner Sunset
address: 1248 9th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA
review:
This place is popping and for good reason—

It was highly recommended by a friend and the flavor of the matcha is rich and authentic tweaked with a little something something like strawberry or pistachio cream. 

Food lives up to the hype as well.
note: Grab a Japanese sando with your matcha. This cafe is known for matcha lattes with optional twists like salted cheese, black sesame, or strawberry cream.

name: Progeny Coffee
address: 1395 22nd St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA
review: I was wowed by the quality of the drinks.  I tried the espresso tonic with orange blossom syrup and my friend had a matcha.  Both drinks had and amazing flavor, i need to come back and try more of their drinks.
note: Although a Colombian specialty coffee company first, matcha second, their blueberry iced matcha latte has developed a cult following.

name: Ceré Tea
address: 1186 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA
review: probably my top spot in sf to get matcha now, beating out past favorites.  the matcha itself is strong, flavorful, and not bitter like some cafes.
note: A Japanese tea room on Valencia with one of the more inventive flavor menus in the city. The osmanthus latte and black sesame einspanner are genuinely unusual finds.

name: Matcha Cafe Maiko
address: 1581 Webster St # 175, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA
aiSummary: Snug shop whipping up matcha-flavored ice cream, lattes and parfaits in house-made waffle cones.
review: My friend and I had the matcha latte and the matcha ice cream, and we loved the intense matcha flavor of both! The soft smooth came with matcha powder and was soft and creamy. The matcha latte was a good balance of oat milk and matcha flavor. The service was fast and the ambiance was casual.
note: Focused primarily on matcha, this is the spot if you're craving dessert too. It has soft serve, parfaits, and shaved ice, with optional toppings like mochi balls, kuromitsu syrup, and adzuki beans.

name: Neighbor's Corner
address: 499 Douglass St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA
aiSummary: Coffee, tea and sandwiches in a relaxed, dog-friendly cafe, plus a warm vibe.
review: Love pretty much everything about this cute place, but definitely I keep coming back for matcha strawberry latte and those mochis!
note: A Japanese-inspired corner cafe in the Castro with tamago sandwiches, daifuku mochi, and matcha alongside a strong coffee program. The kind of neighborhood spot that feels intentional without trying too hard.

name: Black Bird Bookstore and Café
address: 4541 Irving St, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA
aiSummary: Neighborhood bookstore and cafe featuring a wide selection of adult and children's books, as well as a garden.
review: Really good and refreshing. The matcha was smooth and not too bitter, and the strawberries added a nice natural sweetness. You can tell they were fresh and locally sourced, which made a difference.
note: Part bookstore, part café, this cozy spot even has a backyard garden. Its seasonal matcha lattes, like peach and strawberry, are made with farmers market fruit syrups.

name: Compton’s Coffee House
address: 1910 Fillmore St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA
aiSummary: Casual, modern destination for coffee-shop classics (espresso and pastries included) featuring outdoor seating.
review: The raspberry matcha latte was refreshing! The matcha was nice and raspberry syrup was a good pairing!
note: A neighborhood anchor steps from the Japantown Peace Plaza. Compton's is a reliable, well-made matcha latte in a spot that draws a loyal local crowd — expect a line on weekend mornings.

name: Tokyo Cream
address: 1838 Irving St, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA
review: I just noticed that they had opened a Tokyo Cream in the area, and I thought I’d give it a try.  We got to try their matcha, yuzu spritzer, and the banana sundae.  We loved the banana sundae, it had banana pudding and chocolate drizzle.
note: Opened March 2026, this Japanese dessert cafe is built around matcha soft serve, sundaes, and drinks. Pair your matcha with a Kouignigiri, a flaky pastry with in the shape of a Japanese rice ball.

name: Jade & Java Cafe
address: 51 Cambon Dr, San Francisco, CA 94132, USA
review: Their menu is simple to choose from and not vast. They offer matcha drinks, fruit teas, and Vietnamese coffee.  Everything is made to order which is always a plus for me and I never mind the wait since I am a very patient person.
note: A matcha and Vietnamese coffee cafe opened in the Fall of 2025. Standout flavors include the crème brûlée matcha and banana cream matcha.

name: Asha Tea House
address: 17 Kearny St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA
aiSummary: Casual teahouse offering a variety of whole-leaf teas and specialty beverages.
review: Dropped in for the Strawberry Matcha and it was awesome I came back later in the day for another.  Really sweet and beautiful Tea house with nice staff.
note: A short walk from downtown offices, Asha's tranquil decor invites customers in. They specialize in different kinds of tea, and their matcha is regarded highly.

name: Breadbelly
address: 1408 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA
aiSummary: Snug eatery offering Asian-accented baked goods and sandwiches.
review: I LOVE LOVE LOVE breadbelly.  It’s has the BEST matcha lattes in the city in my opinion, even better than some of the matcha I had in Japan, which is A LOT.  I have been trying to find a matcha that tops this place and very few places come close.
note: An Asian-Californian bakery where the matcha latte keeps getting praise on social media. The baked goods are the main draw, but the matcha is known to be great as well.

name: Matcha Cafe Maiko
address: 756 Grant Ave, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA
aiSummary: Dessert cafe specializing in soft-serve ice cream and drinks made from premium Uji matcha.
review: I loved the strawberry sundae! If you enjoy the rich flavor of matcha with the sweetness of strawberry, you will probably love it too. Tip: mix it well at the beginning so the sweetness stays balanced until the last bite.
note: The place to go for all the matcha dessert lovers out there. The matcha soft serve, frappes, floats, and sundaes are popular.

name: Kissaten HiFi
address: 189 6th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA
review: So happy to support a young, Asian-owned business in the neighborhood.  The matcha was delicious (I tried the matcha matcha) the team was incredibly welcoming, the vibes were great, music was stellar.  Definitely bringing a some cool factor to the block.
note: Young Asian-owned shop with a focused Japanese drinks menu. The ichigo matcha is a favorite selection.

name: Tadaima Marina
address: 1849 Union St, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA
review: This is one of my favorite matcha spots! I've been to both locations and I love how this location has more seatings and tables. Their matcha and hojicha lattes are delicious. I can count on my drink to be consistent and tasty every time.
note: The newest Tadaima location in Cow Hollow. Same matcha latte + Japanese sando concept as the other locations.

name: OISHII MATCHA
address: 2299 Market St Suite A, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA
review: We got the strawberry matcha and ube lattes. Both were flavorful and not too sweet at 50% and 25% sugar respectively. The matcha was really high quality and strong.
note: Opened in 2025, this matcha spot has different flavors like ube matcha, strawberry matcha latte, and soft serve.

name: Best Boy Electric
address: 1746 Post St, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA
review: Really yummy hojicha! Super cute space with tote bags and they sell Matcha and other teas! They actually whisk their teas by hand and the staff is super friendly! They have free Wi-Fi and really nice bathrooms and a really great place to work :) love it here and I'm so glad I stumbled upon this place!!

name: Stonemill Matcha
address: 561 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA
aiSummary: Matcha-based beverages & light Japanese bites served in a relaxed, minimalist space.
review: loved their matcha! probably one of the best in the city. the thick an creamy texture makes you want to come back. my favorite is their regular iced matcha and strawberry matcha latte 🍵
note: One of the first dedicated matcha spots in SF, closed during COVID and eventually reopened. Minimalist space, focused menu. The latte is clean and not too sweet.

name: Urban Ritual Cafe
address: 488 Fell St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA
aiSummary: Modern spot for artisanal tea & coffee drinks including boba options a few toast snacks.
review: The creme brûlée as well as the UBE boba are some of the best drinks I’ve had in SF period.  The matcha is also amazing for a boba spot (matcha snob here).  Do yourself a favor and get urban ritual.
note: The creme brulee drink gets all the attention, but the matcha program is solid too. The toffee brittle matcha latte is the unusual order worth trying.

name: Kiss of Matcha
address: 750 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA
review: The matcha drinks and desserts are different than other matcha cafes in the sense that their matcha has *very* strong flavors, including some bitterness. It almost feels like the entire drink or dessert is made of pure matcha and just a little bit milk, cream, or sugar. This place may appeal more to matcha purists than the average matcha consumer.
note: Matcha drinks and frozen yogurt, which sounds like a weird combo but works. The yogurt base brings a tanginess that changes the matcha differently from milk.

name: Maruwu Seicha
address: 1737 Post St #368, San Francisco, CA 94115, USA
aiSummary: Boba, soft-serve, lattes and cheese tarts spot known for matcha treats.
review: Wow, and I mean wow.   The matcha green tea was amazing!   I had it hot, and it was one of the best I ever had.
note: Japanese tea house with a dessert menu that leans hard into matcha. The ube and taro matcha lattes are both good, and the cheese tart is one of the more unusual matcha-based items you will find.

name: SOHN
address: 2535 3rd St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA
review: We ordered the Signature Cream Top Matcha Oat (iced) and the Banana Oat Milk Latte Matcha (iced) the cream top was alright, but the banana oat one was really good. My sister loved it. Overall, the space is beautiful, and if you like working in a calm, aesthetic cafe, this is definitely the spot.
note: Creative flavor pairings you will not find at a standard matcha bar. The banana oat milk matcha and melona latte are the two to know.

name: Kiss of Matcha
address: 612 Broadway, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA
aiSummary: Cafe offering matcha-based drinks and desserts such as soft serve in a casual setting.
review: The matcha drinks and desserts are different than other matcha cafes in the sense that their matcha has *very* strong flavors, including some bitterness. It almost feels like the entire drink or dessert is made of pure matcha and just a little bit milk, cream, or sugar. This place may appeal more to matcha purists than the average matcha consumer.
note: Tucked away and easy to walk past. Same matcha drinks and frozen yogurt format as the Inner Richmond location.

name: Compton’s Coffee House
address: 543 Columbus Ave, San Francisco, CA 94133, USA
aiSummary: Cozy coffee shop with Wi-Fi featuring coffee, tea, pastries, and prepared foods.
review: The raspberry matcha latte was refreshing! The matcha was nice and raspberry syrup was a good pairing!
note: The North Beach outpost brings the same dependable matcha to Columbus Avenue. Cozy, wi-fi friendly, and a solid stop before browsing City Lights.

name: Kiss of Matcha
address: 2127 Irving St, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA
review: Really happy that one of my favorite matcha places in SF opened a new location closer to Ocean Beach. I've gone to their other locations for a long time and had almost all their drinks. My favorite is their strawberry matcha latte and their regular matcha latte (at 50% sweetness). Their matcha tends to be more astringent than some other matcha in the area, so just be warned if that isn't your thing and you prefer a smoother matcha.
note: The Inner Sunset branch of Kiss of Matcha, on Irving Street. Same quality as the Inner Richmond location, with a quieter neighborhood feel. The matcha leans astringent, which regulars tend to appreciate.

name: Matcha Cafe Maiko
address: 3251 20th Ave Ste 250K, San Francisco, CA 94132, USA
aiSummary: Boba, cake, crepe and parfait desserts shop specializing in green-tea soft serve and shaved ice, plus lattes and floats.
review: One of my favorite places to get a matcha vanilla soft serve! Tastes amazing! I also love the matcha latte! You really can't go wrong with anything on the menu!
note: The mall location of the Uji matcha chain. Same soft serve, parfaits, and matcha floats as the other spots, and the only location with actual parking.

name: Shoji
address: 140 New Montgomery St Suite 1, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA
review:
The uni donburi is outstanding: rich, fresh, and perfectly balanced, a must-order if you love sea urchin.  Even the matcha drinks deserve special mention, it easily one of the best matchas in the city. 

Exceptional ingredients, thoughtful execution, and a refined yet welcoming atmosphere.
note: Japanese restaurant with three ways to drink matcha: a negroni, an einspanner, and an affogato. Worth going for the drinks alone, or stay for the food.

name: Moriwa Matcha
address: 1143 Taraval St, San Francisco, CA 94116, USA
review: I got the double cream matcha and it was super rich and creamy ($6.75). The sweetness level was perfect for me and the matcha was not too sweet or bitter — it was really smooth and delicious.
note: A dedicated matcha shop on a quiet stretch of Taraval, Moriwa keeps the menu tight — double cream matcha, strawberry, mango. Still building its reputation, but the regulars it's earning are enthusiastic ones.

name: Brew Cha
address: 3420 18th St #001, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA
aiSummary: Trendy teahouse brewing organic teas and bubble teas, plus offering plush-toy claw machines.
review: Matcha drinks are well balanced, loved the fresh strawberry flavor. Black sesame matcha is SO good. I loved the earthy nutty flavor!
note: A matcha and tea shop that also has claw machines, which is either charming or bizarre depending on your expectations. The matcha is clean and the service is fast.

name: Tadaima Mission
address: 3515 20th St B, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA
aiSummary: Modern cafe for familiar Japanese sandwiches and other eats, plus drinks such as matcha tea.
review:
Just tried the red bean mochi & matcha cream - it's so fun and unusual! 
The matcha is great!
note: The second Tadaima location. The red bean mochi with matcha cream is the unusual order: cold, layered, and nothing like a standard latte.

name: Q Specialty Coffee
address: 3490 California St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA
review:
Even when it’s packed, you’re never waiting long, and the staff keeps everything flowing effortlessly. 

The matcha deserves its own shoutout — perfectly balanced, creamy, and not overly sweet.  You can tell they use high-quality matcha and actually know how to prepare it properly.
note: A rare spot that takes both coffee and matcha seriously. Beans roasted in-house in 12 minutes, six matcha drinks including the Triple Matcha Yuzu Cloud, and Lady M mille crepes if you need something sweet.

name: Komeya No Bento
address: 3137 Laguna St, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA
aiSummary: Japanese restaurant serving comfort food including salmon in truffle soy in a casual atmosphere.
review:
Very busy but we ordered online. Worth the wait and LOVED the bento boxes. Matcha was def a plus
Might need some help with line filtering or keeping the peace, seemed chaotic but perhaps that’s just the weekend matcha rush
note: Primarily a bento shop where the matcha is genuinely good. Order online to skip the wait, and add the strawberry matcha latte to any food order.

name: Nagomi
address: 74 New Montgomery St, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA
review: My new favorite Matcha cafe!  Quality is the name of the game!
note: A newer matcha spot still building its review count. Early visitors consistently praise the matcha quality.
